user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to monitor collection progress, create/update collections, verify items, and manage cataloger permissions (add/remove catalogers from both collections and items), so that I can effectively facilitate the execution of projects.
true_user_story: As a collection manager or cataloger I want to be able to see who last edited a record and when.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to create, update, and delete collections, as well as manage users and items within those collections. This includes verifying items, adding catalogers to both collections and items, and monitoring the progress of collection projects. This will enable me to effectively oversee and execute various tasks related to the repository's collections.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has create/update/delete access to records in my collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to create, update, and delete collections, as well as manage users and items within those collections. This includes verifying items, adding catalogers to both collections and items, and monitoring the progress of collection projects. This will enable me to effectively oversee and execute various tasks related to the repository's collections.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has edit access to individual records in my collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to monitor the progress of collections under my supervision, so that I can effectively facilitate and execute projects.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict access to collection metadata.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to create/update collections, verify items, and manage cataloger permissions, so that I can facilitate the execution of projects and monitor collection progress effectively.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to edit an arbitrary number of Items at once so that I can make changes to all Items in a collection efficiently.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to create and update collections, verify items within those collections, and manage items (create, retrieve, update, delete) so that I can effectively monitor and facilitate the progress of our projects.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to use a grid/spreadsheet style interface so I can edit Items quickly.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items so that I can ensure all necessary metadata records are present and complete. Additionally, if an item contains one or more components, it must also contain a structural metadata record. Each item must have at least one descriptive metadata record. The profile assigned to the item should describe which metadata fields are used and required.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to save a record without all of the required metadata so that I can start a record with the metadata I have and come back to finish it later.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to be able to view incomplete items within a collection, so that I can ensure all metadata records are up-to-date and complete.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to save a record only when the record has an identifier so that we don't end up with a bunch of empty records in the collection.

user_story: As a Limited Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items within my assigned collections, so that I can update and complete the metadata records for those items.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to publish records only when the record has a Title, Identifier, Type, and

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to be able to view incomplete items, verify metadata records, create/edit/delete records, manage cataloger permissions, and create/edit collection-level metadata, so that I can effectively maintain the repository and ensure accurate record keeping.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want a list of all of the records I have access to so I can get to work quickly.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and report problems with items or collections, so that I can facilitate the execution of projects effectively.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to search for records based on status so I can track the progress of a collection.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view all incomplete items within specific collections or categories, so that I can ensure the completeness and accuracy of metadata records.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to search for records based on all Duke Core fields.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to create, update, and delete collections, so that I can manage the organization of items within the repository effectively.
true_user_story: As an end user I want to be able to search across all collections at once.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want a UI that supports creating, updating, and deleting collections, so that I can effectively manage all collections in the repository.
true_user_story: As a cataloger and a collections manager I want Trident to support Duke Core so that metadata is consistent between collections.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to add catalogers to both Collections and Items, so that I can effectively facilitate the execution of projects by ensuring proper monitoring and progress tracking.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want required DC fields to be enforced so that all records have values for all required DC fields. ♦

user_story: As a librarian, I want to be able to create and update collections, so that I can organize our library's resources effectively. Additionally, I need to verify items within these collections to ensure accuracy. Furthermore, I should be able to manage items by creating, retrieving, updating, or deleting them as needed. It would also be beneficial if I could add catalogers to both the collection and individual items for better collaboration. Lastly, I require guidance on which authority lists to use for specific metadata fields, along with the ability to validate, build, edit, display, and provide guidance on Digital Metadata Records (DMR).
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want to be able to repeat repeatable DC Fields so that I can enter all of the relevant metadata. ♦

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items, so that I can complete and maintain metadata records for items in the repository.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want Optional DC fields to be available but not required so that my collection can decide to use them or not. ♦

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items within specific collections that I have access to, so that I can efficiently maintain and complete metadata records for those items.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to be able to create controlled vocabularies so that they can be used as authority lists during metadata creation.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to be able to view incomplete items, so that I can ensure all necessary metadata fields are populated and validated according to the specified authority lists.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want authority lists to be easily accessible and contextually relevant so it is easy to use the authority.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items and be granted access to records in specific collections, so that I can create and maintain metadata records for items in the repository. Additionally, I need to ensure that each item is a member of at most one collection, which may contain multiple categories and have an associated metadata profile.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to use external authority lists so I they can be used during metadata creation.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items so that I can ensure all necessary metadata is included and complete the cataloging process efficiently.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read the digital object I am cataloging so I don't have to move between multiple applications while I'm creating metadata.

user_story: As a librarian, I want to view incomplete items so that I can ensure all necessary components are included and complete the digitization process efficiently.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read all components associated on the with an item on the create/update screen so I don't have to leave the form.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items so that I can verify and complete their metadata records.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to see all of the metadata for a record while I'm editing it so that I have the full context for the item I'm cataloging.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and report problems with items or collections, so that I can facilitate the execution of projects effectively.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want each record to have a status so I can track the progress of the records in the collection.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to monitor the progress of collections, so that I can ensure projects are executed efficiently and on time.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want statuses to include a status that flags a record as having a problem so we can track issues as the arise.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to add component files to Trident and create administrative metadata for each component, so that I can effectively manage and organize the system's resources. Additionally, I need to create component derivatives to ensure that all necessary variations are available for use by different user roles.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want Trident to support the UTF-8 character set so that I can use any character necessary when creating metadata.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to validate and build DMRs (Descriptive Metadata Records) on the editing screen, so that I can accurately describe items according to the specified metadata profiles and authority lists. Additionally, I need to ensure that each item contains one or more descriptive metadata records and, if it has components, a structural metadata record as well. This will help in providing clear guidance to end users when displaying the DMRs.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to map fields in inherited metadata to Duke Core fields and import a spreadsheet and create records.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be provided with guidance on which authority lists should be used for each metadata field when creating or editing a Descriptive Metadata Record (DMR), so that the DMR is validated and conforms to the specified Metadata Profile.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to import metadata and create records from a DCP digitazation guide.

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a librarian, I want to be able to link an image to a book page and vice versa, so that users can easily navigate between related content.
true_user_story: As a Archivist I want bi-directional linking between items in the digital collections and EAD finding aids

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a librarian, I want to be able to add and manage books in our digital library system, so that users can access and browse through various books easily. Each book should contain descriptive metadata such as title, author, publication date, and one or more page components which could be images of the book's pages.
true_user_story: As a End User I want to be able to see the physical context of an Item in a digital collection (i.e. See inside this folder; EAD)

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to create/update collections, verify items, and manage cataloger permissions, so that I can facilitate the execution of projects and monitor collection progress effectively.
true_user_story: As a Collection Manager I want my collection to be discoverable in the Catalog

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to be able to group scannable objects into components, check their quality and correctness, and oversee the digitization process handled by Scanner Operators, so that the digital content is accurately and efficiently produced.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU) I want to be able to create Components.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to add component files to Trident and create administrative metadata for each component, so that all components are properly documented and associated with a Metadata Profile. Additionally, I need to create component derivatives and ensure that each item contains one or more descriptive metadata records. If an item has 1 or more components, it must also contain a structural metadata record.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Technical Metadata with for Component.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to add component files to Trident and create administrative metadata for each component, so that all components are properly documented and associated with a Metadata Profile. Additionally, I need to create component derivatives and ensure that each item contains one or more descriptive metadata records. If an item has 1 or more components, it must also contain a structural metadata record.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Preservation Metadata for a Component.

user_story: As a librarian, I want to add component files to Trident and create administrative metadata for each component, so that we can accurately catalog and manage our digital content. Additionally, I need to be able to create component derivatives, define scanning specifications, scan materials, and perform CRUD operations on both items and components. Furthermore, I should have the capability to add scanner operators to the system or specific collections, ensuring efficient workflow management.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to upload master surrogates to a Component.

user_story: As a librarian, I want to be able to add multiple components (such as images, text documents, or PDFs) to an item in the digital repository, so that users can access and explore different parts of the same content seamlessly.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to add one or more Components to an Item.

user_story: As a data manager, I want to ensure that every item containing one or more components also includes a structural metadata record, so that our data remains organized and easily navigable.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create structural metadata for an Item so I can define the sequence of components.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to manage scanner operators, perform quality control assessments of scans, and add components to items, so that the digitization process runs smoothly and efficiently.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production Developer (DPD) I want to group components that require digitization so that I can create "digitizable units".

user_story: As a Scanner Operator, I want to be able to digitize materials, add components to an item, and create technical metadata, so that I can ensure the quality and correctness of the scanned items.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to check-out/check-in groups of Components.

user_story: As a Scanner Operator, I want to be able to digitize materials, add components to an item, and create technical metadata, so that I can ensure the quality and correctness of the scanned items.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to verify the correctness and completeness of components so that I can ensure quality standards are met.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to be able to identify items and components for digitization, create scanning specifications, scan materials, create/retrieve/update items, create/retrieve/update/delete components, and add scanner operators to the system and/or collection. This will allow me to efficiently run the digitization process, ensuring high-quality digital outputs and smooth workflow management.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to capture digitization statistics so that I can monitor Operator efficiency and make projections for future projects.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to add component files to Trident and create administrative metadata for each component, so that I can effectively manage and organize the system's resources. Additionally, I need to create component derivatives to ensure that all necessary variations are available for use by different user roles.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want Trident to create derivatives for me when I upload a Master.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and report problems with items or collections, so that I can facilitate the execution of projects effectively.
true_user_story: As a User I want to monitor the progress of a digital collection project.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to be able to identify items and components for digitization, create scanning specifications, scan materials, create/retrieve/update items, create/retrieve/update/delete components, and add scanner operators to the system and/or collection. This will allow me to efficiently run the digitization process, ensuring high-quality digital outputs and smooth workflow management.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been digitized.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to manage scanner operators, perform quality control assessments of scans, and add components to items, so that I can efficiently run the digitization process.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many digitized Items have been described.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to view incomplete items and monitor collection progress, so that I can ensure all tasks are completed on time.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been published.♦

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to be able to monitor collection progress, so that I can effectively facilitate the execution of projects.
true_user_story: As Collection Manager or Sponsor I want to describe the collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to use predefined authority lists for metadata fields when validating, building, editing, and displaying DMRs, so that the data is consistent and accurate. Additionally, I need guidance on how to properly catalog items.
true_user_story: As a Preservation Librarian I want the relationship between masters and derivatives to be managed by the system so I don't have to.

user_story: As a librarian, I want to be able to assign an image or book item to at most one collection, so that I can maintain organized and structured metadata records for each item.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define the metadata schema for a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to select authority lists for metadata fields when validating, building, editing, and displaying DMRs, so that the correct and consistent terminology is used across all items in a collection. Additionally, I want guidance on which metadata profile to use for an item, so that the required fields are accurately filled out and the item is properly categorized within its collection.
true_user_story: As a Metadata librarian I want to define Authorities to be used for specific fields in a collection MD Schema.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the system to validate metadata using predefined rules, so that I can ensure the accuracy and consistency of descriptive information.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define validation rules for specific fields in a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the system to validate metadata using predefined rules, so that I can ensure the accuracy and completeness of descriptive information.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want validation rules enforced when MD is submitted.
